message:
fix: add .beads-credential-key to .gitignore template (PR #2681)

The encryption key file .beads-credential-key was missing from the
.beads/.gitignore template written by bd init, allowing it to be
accidentally committed to git.

- Add .beads-credential-key to GitignoreTemplate
- Add .beads-credential-key to requiredPatterns (bd doctor check)
- Update NoSensitivePatterns test to allow this known legitimate entry
- Add regression tests to prevent future removal

Closes #2671

Co-Authored-By: Chris Deal <cdeal@mines.edu>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -32,6 +32,9 @@ push-state.json
 # Lock files (various runtime locks)
 *.lock
 
+# Credential key (encryption key for federation peer auth — never commit)
+.beads-credential-key
+
 # Local version tracking (prevents upgrade notification spam after git ops)
 .local_version
 
@@ -109,6 +112,7 @@ var requiredPatterns = []string{
 	"interactions.jsonl",
 	"*.lock",
 	"*.corrupt.backup/",
+	".beads-credential-key",
 }
 
 // CheckGitignore checks if .beads/.gitignore is up to date.
